title: Supporting the designation of May 2025 as "National Myositis Awareness Month".
plain_summary: This bill wants to make May 2025 a special month called National Myositis Awareness Month. Myositis is a group of rare diseases where the body's immune system attacks its own muscles by mistake. The bill encourages all Americans to learn more about these diseases and help support families who are dealing with them. The goal is to raise awareness so more people understand what myositis is and how it affects people.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution supports making May 2025 National Myositis Awareness Month.

sections.title: Background Facts
sections.text: People in the United States are affected by myositis and other rare diseases. Nearly one in ten Americans have rare diseases. Myositis refers to a group of rare, long-lasting diseases where the body's immune system attacks its own muscles. These diseases cause muscle wasting, painful muscle swelling, tiredness, trouble swallowing, and can also damage the lungs. The myositis group includes several specific diseases with complicated names like dermatomyositis, inclusion body myositis, and polymyositis. More research is needed to find out what causes these diseases and how to treat them. Myositis is hard for doctors to diagnose, so patients often wait a long time to get proper treatment and have trouble finding doctors who know about their condition. All people with myositis have a lower quality of life because there is no cure and these diseases can shorten life, especially inclusion body myositis. Women and people of color with myositis face even greater health challenges. Public awareness and education campaigns about myositis happen during the month of May.

sections.title: What the House of Representatives Resolves
sections.text: The House of Representatives supports making May 2025 National Myositis Awareness Month. The House recognizes how important it is to raise awareness about myositis and how it affects veterans and citizens of the United States. The House encourages all people in the United States to learn more about myositis and to support the individuals and families who are affected by these conditions.
